data.epi_content:
# "Ah."

I suddenly rose from my seat, feeling overwhelmed.

Looking down at my body, I wasn't burning.

Yet, the image of myself burning wouldn't fade, so I looked in the mirror.

A girl with stern features, yet beautiful, reflected back at me.

"Damn pistol, pistol."

I stole a body.

Or more accurately, perhaps I stole a corpse.

Maybe that's why I'm being punished.

Because I'm inside a human who must die in a few days.

Being alive is the problem.

Would dying solve everything?

Anyway, I rummage through the drawer.

What I find is a pistol with an ivory grip adorned with fancy gold decorations.

The barrel is disgustingly short, but this magnificent powder weapon in my hand could easily kill a person.

Those Blacks who work on plantations getting whipped do their jobs well, and this thing does its job well too.

Considering the number of heads it's blown off so far, it must be in the double digits... or maybe not, but the bullets certainly fly well.

Of course, no bullet has been fired yet, but still.

Each time I load a bullet, there's a distinctive metallic friction sound.

Each time I rotate the barrel, it makes a click-clack sound.

When I press the muzzle against my forehead, all I hear is the soft sound of skin being pressed.

With only five bullets loaded, the probability of dying is 5/6.

Infinitely low.

Should I put one finger on the trigger?

I'm trembling terribly.

So much that my teeth feel cold.

"Hoo, right, f...uck. Fuck. That's it."

As always, this lady's mouth seems to only allow refined language, so even curses don't come out properly.

I thought I'd gotten rid of enough of that.

Damn déjà vu.

If I shoot like this, like last time, I'll just end up with a hole in the back of my neck.

I'll probably drown in my own blood, dying slowly, miserably, and painfully.

I might die painfully while still alive, or someone might treat me and I might not die at all.

Was there such a time? I don't remember clearly.

I raise the muzzle so it slightly grazes the roof of my mouth, hold the trigger with my left hand, and roughly aim at my head with my right.

Then, I close my eyes.

The trigger...

Click.

"Ha!"

No bullet fired.

Checking inside, only five rounds are loaded.

A revelation. A revelation to live.

A revelation from myself.

"Disgustingly stale."

With the peculiar taste of metal powder that's somewhat bitter, I spit out the saliva pooled in my mouth onto the floor.

I toss the pistol I was holding onto the desk and rub my face.

Every day was hell.

Especially without opportunity.

But, thinking the other way, perhaps I simply haven't sought out opportunities myself.

What have I accomplished besides being powerlessly confined to my room, whining and cursing my fate?
data.epi_content2:
There is no God.

If there were, He wouldn't have left me like this.

Unless He wanted to cast me into hell.

I've heard many times that humans are so foolish that even their reason isn't worthy of worship, but in a situation like this, what else is there to believe in besides oneself?

Moreover, to move forward, I truly have only myself to believe in.

Just because I take a step forward doesn't guarantee that the person beside me will take the same step.

"Where's the cigar... ah."

Come to think of it, I needed to buy tobacco.

Since I don't have a pipe to put tobacco leaves in, I'll end up buying expensive cigars.

Well, they smell and taste good, so it's not a problem.

Still feeling regretful, I put my hand in my pocket and find half a cigar that was clearly half-smoked and roughly stubbed out.

"Wait a moment."

And soon after, the tobacco I was holding crumbles and scatters into the air.

"Is it a hallucination?"

Time also feels subtly distorted.

Last time, there were definitely teacups and a teapot prepared on the table where I was sitting.

No, there's no problem at all.

The tea leaves, teacups, teapot, and cigars were surely brought from Mecklenburg, so there's absolutely no problem.

It seems everything from Mecklenburg, like this lady or this cigar, is defective.

"Stop making such a gloomy expression and try smiling."

At that, the girl looking in the mirror smiles brightly.

Perhaps because my entire body burned once, I roughly know where each muscle is.

The memory of parts that didn't move due to contraction from burning remains.

Even I think this is complete nonsense.

Her face was stiff and barely moved, but when did she become capable of such a radiant smile?

It must be because I burned away a life full of pain, boredom, and sorrow.

Such a thing never happened even when I was shot, bled to death, or had my head blown off, so perhaps burning people to death during witch hunts was unexpectedly necessary.

Soon, Vivian will come to my room.

I don't want to see her face.

I don't want to smell her distinctive pleasant scent.

I don't want to see her precious spells.

I hate her dullness, how she genuinely worries about me but fails to notice what the real problem is.

Whatever path I take, I hate seeing Vivian succeed.

Until I burned, I had been living with the hopeful thought that I was happy and gradually improving.

Ignoring how I writhed, begging to be saved because being near you felt like imminent death.

If that detestable Crown Prince hadn't handed me the potion and powder to burn to death, how would I have died?

I probably would have rushed to the window during class and tried to throw myself out, making sure to land head first.

With luck, I could have died instantly.

But I don't go as far as to resent him.

I just dislike Vivian as a person.

What's there to like or dislike about it?

And every time I meet someone, if I alone harbor grudges, anger, hatred, and express sorrow over things I can't even remember, the only one who becomes strange is me.

I didn't want to expend my mental energy on such things anymore.

I thought the mind was an infinite resource, but as time passed, I realized it was finite.

"I should go out for some fresh air."

Soon, Evan will come looking for me.

Maybe, like last time, he'll come in a changed state.

With that thought, I get up, grab a few gold coins from the drawer, and head outside.

"Erica, shall we head to the classroom?

You don't have to attend class today!"

Vivian stands before me with her hands behind her back, leaning slightly forward in a posture that reveals her chest, and asks me a question.

I need to escape. Otherwise, like last time, Vivian will cling to me for a month or two until I die.

No, do I really need to escape?

I take out the pistol from my bosom, aim it straight ahead, and as I'm about to pull the trigger...
data.epi_content3:
"Aaaaaack!! I'm sorry! Whatever it is, I'm sorry!!"

Suddenly, everything blurs, and a rude little maid sits on the floor, having wet herself.

"Ah, I'm sorry. I mistook you for someone else."

I say, putting the pistol behind my back.

The little lady, with trembling eyes, can't respond and just looks at me fearfully.

Last time, she looked at me like a friend, but after experiencing this, she won't be able to chat like before.

I was secretly enjoying it.

I finally understand how those people locked in mental institutions felt.

It's not like they'd crawl under the bed in shock if someone clapped, but I almost killed someone just now.

Just for the trivial reason that she overlapped with Vivian in my mind.

I walk to the specialty goods store as usual and browse the items.

I pick up a tea leaf that the Crown Prince recommended last time and a box of cigars, and as I walk to the clerk to pay, someone taps my shoulder lightly.

Turning around, it was the Crown Prince.

I greet him with appropriate courtesy and turn back to the clerk, trying to leave immediately.

But when spoken to, I couldn't just walk away; I had to respond.

He was a high and mighty person, after all.

"That tea leaf, if you mix it properly with this one, it will be more helpful."

"Ah, Your Highness. I don't have enough money."

"Well, it's enough if I buy it for you."

He takes the box of cigars and tea leaves I was holding, along with the tea leaves he recommended, hands them to the clerk, and pays.

Then he asks for some of my time in return.

And as it has been in this repeating time, he takes me to a bench by a pond where carps swim in circles.

I bite into a cigar, light it, and try to cough.

Since it's my first time smoking.

Only slight fake coughs come out, and if my breath gets blocked, I just let it be blocked.

As if mere spicy smoke couldn't make me cough.

He too fills his pipe with tobacco and lights it.

"Perhaps today is the day. Your family has been completely destroyed."

"Is that so?"

"So what I'm saying is..."

I don't want to hear more.

I dislike what I just did in my room, and I dislike pointing a gun at the little one who was cleaning my room.

But the world doesn't listen just because I dislike something.

Rather, it tramples on you if it can.

I stole a body.

That's why I'm being punished.

Even if I wanted to return it, the owner wouldn't take it back.

"Just shut up now."

I cut off the Crown Prince's words.

He merely twitches an eyebrow once, says nothing, and continues smoking his tobacco before speaking.

"Did you know? I thought it was just a flower grown for decoration."

"I've heard your bullshit many times, you fucking bastard."

"Your language is harsh."

He looked relaxed, as if he already knew what I was going to do.

He just crosses his legs, puffs on his tobacco, and looks at me with eyes that seem to be looking at an inanimate object.

"Handle it."

I take out my pistol, aim at him, and immediately pull the trigger.

...

When I opened my eyes, I was in my room.
